task: Deep Plan: System-Wide Standardization — Ecosystem Mapping
current_batch: 4T (Tenets Discovery — inserted between Batch 4 and Batch 5)
estimated_remaining_batches: 2-3 (Batch 5: Ecosystem Catalog + Maturity Assessments, Batch 6: Sequencing + Prioritization, possible Batch 7: Edge Cases/Final)

decisions.choice: `.canon/` at repo root — new top-level directory
decisions.rationale: Meta-system deserves top-level visibility, not buried under .planning/ or docs/

decisions.choice: Registry JSONL (`.canon/ecosystems.jsonl`) + per-ecosystem detail files
decisions.rationale: JSONL = canonical/AI-consumed, MD = human views generated from JSONL. Core tenet of the system moving forward.

decisions.choice: Hybrid: completeness tier checklist drives computed level (L0-L5)
decisions.rationale: Makes 'what's missing' obvious and progress measurable. Concrete capabilities compute to a level.

decisions.choice: Per-ecosystem files are source of truth + generated matrix view
decisions.rationale: Follows JSONL-first → generated-views pattern. Each ecosystem knows its own subsystems, matrix is computed.

decisions.choice: Global defaults + per-ecosystem cascade/override (CSS-like)
decisions.rationale: Avoids 70+ hardcoded values while letting ecosystems specialize where needed.

decisions.choice: Dependency graph (CANON first) with maturity as tiebreaker (highest first)
decisions.rationale: Build momentum: early wins validate the framework before tackling hard stuff. CANON → near-compliant → major overhauls.

decisions.choice: Full ecosystem catalog + maturity assessments + sequenced plan + CANON spec
decisions.rationale: We have the research (500+ components). Comprehensive output avoids another discovery pass.

decisions.choice: Approved scale: L0=Nonexistent, L1=Identified, L2=Structured, L3=Monitored, L4=Enforced, L5=Canonized
decisions.rationale: Clear progression. L5 = all applicable subsystems canonized (not all 16 if some don't apply to ecosystem).

sub_decisions.l5_for_everything: L5 is the north star, not the exit criteria. Most ecosystems target L3-L4 baseline. L5 means all *applicable* subsystems canonized — per-ecosystem required-vs-optional mapping.
sub_decisions.plan_structure: Plan-as-you-go with sequenced placeholders. This deep plan produces catalog + sequence. Each ecosystem gets its own deep-plan when its turn comes. Pre-planning all 13 now would produce stale plans by ecosystem #8.

decisions.choice: 16-item checklist (original 13 + 3 gap-fills: inter-ecosystem contracts, rollback/recovery, deprecation policy)
decisions.rationale: User liked original 13, accepted 3 additions to close gaps.
checklist: 1. Zod schemas — for all data structures
2. JSONL storage — canonical data in JSONL format
3. Generated views — MD/JSON views computed from JSONL
4. Health monitoring — at least one health checker with metrics
5. Enforcement manifest — rules defining what's blocking vs warning
6. Testing — unit + integration tests for ecosystem scripts
8. State persistence — survives compaction / session boundaries
10. Naming compliance — follows CANON naming conventions
11. Configuration — uses central config (not hardcoded values)
12. Lifecycle hooks — creation, update, archival, deletion defined
14. Inter-ecosystem contracts — interfaces between dependent ecosystems
15. Rollback/recovery — revert to prior state after bad migration
16. Deprecation policy — how old schemas/APIs/patterns are sunset

decisions.choice: Pattern-level: CANON defines interface + reference pattern (PR Review v2 as exemplar)
decisions.rationale: Templates too rigid, interface-only too ambiguous. Reference patterns formalize what already exists.

decisions.choice: Primary ownership + consumer references (no superseding ecosystem)
decisions.rationale: Primary = maintenance responsibility, not authority hierarchy. Shared components have one owner, many consumers. CANON defines rules but doesn't own other ecosystems' components.

decisions.decision: CANON spec scope in this plan
decisions.choice: Working draft (structure + maturity model + subsystem interfaces + registry schema) with room for growth built in everywhere
decisions.rationale: Enough to start building CANON. Ecosystem assessments separate. Room for growth is a core tenet — nothing ships as a dead end.

sub_decisions.upgradeability: Core tenet: nothing not-upgradeable. Ease the upgrade process. Everything must have room for growth. Things change all the time.

decisions.choice: Semver (start at 0.1.0, promote to 1.0.0 after 2-3 ecosystem validations)
decisions.rationale: CANON is infrastructure others depend on. Breaking changes need explicit migration guidance.

decisions.choice: 4-tier: error (blocking), warning (non-blocking + logged), info (displayed only), silent (suppressed)
decisions.rationale: Granular enough without being complex. Silent tier handles known-accepted items.

decisions.choice: Tiered: pre-commit (fast checks), pre-push (full validation), PR (comprehensive)
decisions.rationale: Fast feedback for quick checks, thorough validation before sharing. Each gate has appropriate scope.

decisions.choice: Files + exports + JSONL fields (3-layer naming standard)
decisions.rationale: Covers the surfaces that matter most. Full taxonomy is overkill, files-only misses the data layer.

decisions.choice: Current interface + trend data + recommendations (enhanced)
decisions.rationale: Trend enables progress tracking over time. Recommendations make health checks actionable, not just diagnostic.

decisions.choice: Hybrid: auto-generate on data change + on-demand for full rebuild
decisions.rationale: Stale dashboards are useless, but full rebuild on every change is wasteful. Incremental auto + manual full.

decisions.choice: Pilot + rollout: validate with 2-3 near-compliant ecosystems, then roll out to rest
decisions.rationale: Validates CANON itself before forcing it on complex ecosystems. Early pilots surface spec issues cheaply.

decisions.choice: Both: per-ecosystem declares own deps + central registry aggregates all
decisions.rationale: Per-ecosystem is source of truth (consistent with Decision #4). Central view is generated/computed for cross-cutting analysis.

decisions.choice: Hybrid: CANON infrastructure in categorized dirs + per-ecosystem dirs for assessment/detail data
decisions.rationale: Best of both worlds. CANON's own config/schemas/reports organized by function. Each ecosystem's data co-located in its own dir.

structure..canon/tenets.jsonl: Core tenets (first CANON artifact — CANON defines itself first)

decisions.choice: Extensible core: required core fields + optional fields with defaults. Schema validates required, accepts extra.
decisions.rationale: Registry line is summary/index. Detail files have full picture. Room for growth — new fields without breaking existing data.

decisions.choice: Per-ecosystem files: `.canon/ecosystems/{id}/assessment.jsonl` — co-located with ecosystem data
decisions.rationale: Keeps ecosystem data co-located (consistent with Decision #4). Each ecosystem owns its assessment. Matrix/summary views generated.

decisions.choice: Zod as source of truth → JSON Schema auto-generated for external tooling. JSONL tracks implementation status.
decisions.rationale: Follows established pattern: source of truth (Zod) → generated views (JSON Schema). Plus JSONL tracks adoption per ecosystem.

decisions.choice: JSON summary envelope (score, trend, metadata) + JSONL findings stream (one finding per line)
decisions.rationale: Summary is single object for dashboards. Individual findings are JSONL (appendable, processable). Best of both.

decisions.choice: Single JSONL file per ecosystem, each rule has `tier` + `severity` fields
decisions.rationale: Single file easier to maintain/query. Tier (pre-commit/pre-push/PR) + severity (error/warning/info/silent from #14) per rule. Tools filter by tier at runtime.

decisions.choice: Standardized script interface: each view is a script with contract (reads JSONL → outputs MD). Standard CLI interface (--input, --output).
decisions.rationale: Standardized interface, not implementation. Scripts can be simple or complex — the contract is what's standardized. Follows Decision #10 pattern-level standardization.

decisions.decision: Core tenets discovery phase — inserted before Batch 5
decisions.choice: Pause schema batches. Discover, formalize, and lock all core tenets BEFORE continuing with ecosystem assessments.
decisions.rationale: Core tenets guide everything downstream. Locking schemas before fully articulating the principles that shape them is backwards. Two tenets (automation over discipline, no forgettable processes) had already fallen through the cracks, proving the need.

decisions.decision: Tenet candidate disposition — promote, merge, demote
decisions.choice: Promote 6 candidates to formal tenets, merge 2 into existing tenets, demote 2 to implementation patterns.
decisions.rationale: Tenets are 'why' principles that guide decisions. Patterns are 'how' implementations. Cascade/override (D#5) and progressive disclosure are patterns, not principles.

merged.nothing_gets_lost: Merged into renamed tenet 'capture_everything_surface_what_matters' (combines visibility + institutional memory)
merged.discoveries_feed_forward: Merged as sub-principle of 'plan_as_you_go' (knowledge compounds, learnings from ecosystem #3 inform #4)

demoted.cascade_override: Implementation pattern (Decision #5), not a guiding principle. Referenced by tenets but isn't one.
demoted.progressive_disclosure: UX/design guideline for tooling. L0-L5 and tiered gates implement it, but it's not why they exist.

decisions.choice: Add platform_agnostic_by_default, idempotent_operations, fail_loud_fail_early. Formalize automation_over_discipline (was discussed but never recorded).
decisions.rationale: Platform-agnostic: dual-environment (Linux sandbox + Windows CLI) is a real constraint with existing breakage. Idempotency: prevents data corruption from re-runs (MASTER_DEBT race condition is a violation). Fail loud/early: complements visibility — errors must scream, not whisper. Automation over discipline: if it relies on human memory, it WILL fail.

dual_environment_impact.implications: All CANON scripts Node.js (not bash). Forward-slash paths. LF line endings. No symlinks. No platform-specific env vars without cross-platform fallback.
existing_breakage: 3 bash-only scripts have no Windows equivalent

decisions.choice: Option A: Flat list with 4 categories (Foundation, Design, Operations, Process). No parent-child hierarchy.
decisions.rationale: At 16 tenets, a flat list becomes a wall. Categories make it scannable. No hierarchy avoids 'is X a child of Y?' debates. Easy to add new tenets to a category.

decisions.choice: `.canon/tenets.jsonl` (source of truth) + `.canon/tenets.md` (generated view). First artifact CANON produces about itself.
decisions.rationale: CANON defines itself first. Tenets depend on nothing else. Dog-foods JSONL-first pattern. Each line: {id, category, name, statement, evidence[], added, version}.

schema.name: snake_case identifier
schema.evidence: Array of decision IDs that informed/embody this tenet

core_tenets._organization: Flat list, 4 categories. 16 total tenets. Locked in Batch 4T (Tenets Discovery).

T1_canon_is_ecosystem_zero.statement: CANON is the meta-system above all others. It defines the rules, lives at `.canon/` repo root, and is the first ecosystem standardized.

T2_source_of_truth_generated_views.statement: Every system has ONE authoritative source. Everything else is derived/generated. Never maintain two copies — maintain one and compute the other.
T2_source_of_truth_generated_views.note: JSONL-first (T4) is an instance of this broader principle.

T3_maturity_is_measurable.statement: Maturity is computed from a concrete checklist, never subjectively assigned. 16 items → L0-L5 levels. If you can't measure it, you can't improve it.

T4_jsonl_first.statement: JSONL is the canonical storage format — AI-consumed, appendable, line-diffable. MD is generated for human consumption. Instance of T2.

T5_contract_over_implementation.statement: CANON defines the contract (what a health checker must output, what an enforcement manifest looks like). How each ecosystem implements it is their business.

T6_room_for_growth.statement: Nothing ships as a dead end. Everything must be upgradeable. Ease the upgrade process. Schemas, APIs, structures — all must accommodate evolution without breaking.

T7_platform_agnostic_by_default.statement: All CANON artifacts, scripts, and tooling MUST work identically on both Claude Code Desktop (Linux sandbox) and Windows CLI. Node.js over bash. Forward-slash paths. LF line endings. No platform-specific assumptions without a documented cross-platform fallback.

constraints: Cross-platform hooks (already Node.js — formalize as requirement)
known_violations: 3 bash-only scripts with no Windows equivalent

T8_automation_over_discipline.statement: If a process relies on human memory, it WILL fail. Hooks, gates, scripts — not checklists and READMEs. Automate enforcement or accept non-compliance.
T8_automation_over_discipline.note: Was discussed in Batch 3 but never formally recorded. Formalized in Batch 4T.

T9_crash_proof_state.statement: State survives compaction, session boundaries, crashes, and network failures. Not optional — it's infrastructure. State files, not memory. Persistent, not ephemeral.
T9_crash_proof_state.note: The existence of deep-plan.state.json itself embodies this tenet.

T10_validate_before_scaling.statement: Pilot on 1-2, prove it works, then roll out. Never mass-apply an unproven pattern. CANON validates itself before it standardizes others.

T11_fail_loud_fail_early.statement: When something is wrong, it screams immediately — not silently logs to a file nobody reads. Pre-commit catches before PR. Errors block, they don't whisper. The aggressive complement to visibility.

T12_idempotent_operations.statement: Every CANON script and operation produces the same result whether run once or five times. No data corruption from re-runs. No side effects from retries. Safe to re-run is safe to automate.
T12_idempotent_operations.note: The MASTER_DEBT 9-writer race condition is a direct violation of this tenet.

T13_plan_as_you_go.statement: Each ecosystem gets its own deep-plan when sequenced. No stale pre-plans. Discoveries feed forward — what you learn standardizing ecosystem #3 informs ecosystem #4. Knowledge compounds.
T13_plan_as_you_go.sub_principle: discoveries_feed_forward — merged here from candidate T16

T14_capture_everything_surface_what_matt.statement: Ideas, findings, tangential thoughts MUST be recorded — institutional memory. Then surface what's actionable through visibility tooling. Nothing gets lost AND nothing gets buried.
T14_capture_everything_surface_what_matt.note: Renamed from 'visibility_is_key'. Merges visibility + institutional memory (candidate T14 'nothing gets lost').

T15_interactivity_first.statement: Interactive workflows over batch output. Batch questioning over monologue dumps. User-driven decisions, not AI-driven assumptions. Deep-plan is the exemplar — extends to all skills/tools.

T16_single_ownership_many_consumers.statement: Every component has exactly one owner responsible for it. Others consume, never supersede. Primary ownership = maintenance responsibility, not authority hierarchy.

T17_declarative_over_imperative.statement: Declare WHAT should be, let tools enforce HOW. Enforcement manifests, schemas, configs — all declarative. You describe the world you want; automation makes it so.

user_directives.roadmap_will_change: ROADMAP.md will need additions, changes, removals after this process
user_directives.grand_plan_reassessment: TDMS Grand Plan needs reassessment — changing many files invalidates existing debt items
user_directives.state_persistence_standard: Constant state file saving is a standard here AND repo-wide going forward — to be canonized as rules
user_directives.capture_all_ideas: Stream-of-consciousness ideas MUST be captured even if not immediately actionable
user_directives.canon_is_ecosystem_zero: CANON is the meta-system/Ecosystem Zero — building block for all others
user_directives.overlap_management: Many points of overlap between ecosystems — must not let one supersede another
user_directives.duplicate_subsystems: Cross-cutting subsystems exist in multiple ecosystems and need mapping
user_directives.standardization_is_key: Duplicate subsystems must follow shared standards
user_directives.pr_creep_guard: User has tendency toward 30-50 commit PRs. Wants guardrail mechanism SOON.
user_directives.lifecycle_management: User agreed — helps future-proof. Added to cross-cutting subsystems.
user_directives.jsonl_is_canon: JSONL is canonical/AI-consumed, MD is human-readable generated from JSONL. Core tenet.
user_directives.room_for_growth_everywhere: Nothing not-upgradeable. Ease upgrade process. Room for growth built in everywhere. Things change all the time.
user_directives.no_superseding_ecosystem: Primary ownership = maintenance responsibility, not authority. No ecosystem supersedes another.
user_directives.plan_as_you_go: Each ecosystem gets its own deep-plan when sequenced. Don't pre-plan all 13 — they'd go stale.
user_directives.ecosystem_plan_sequencing_matters: Dependency-wise sequencing between ecosystem plans is important. Discoveries in one plan may feed into another.
user_directives.mcp_memory_enabled: enableAllProjectMcpServers flipped to true. Verify memory MCP tools available next session.
user_directives.interactivity_is_paramount: Interactivity of utmost importance across everything whenever possible. Skills should be interactive (deep-plan as exemplar), not monologue dumps. Extends beyond skills to all methods/tools where applicable.
user_directives.dual_environment_is_constraint: System must work identically on Claude Code Desktop (Linux sandbox) and Windows CLI. Platform-agnostic by default. Node.js over bash. Formalized as tenet T7.

ideas_captured: ROADMAP entries will change significantly post-standardization
Grand Plan debt items may become invalid as files change
State persistence should use whatever resources are best
Future idea capture mechanism needed
Cross-ecosystem subsystem mapping is its own discovery task
Canon is Ecosystem Zero — all others derive from it
Planning standardization (deep-plan, GSD) as backbone — build from there
PR creep guardrail: commit counter hook (warn 10, block 25, override available)
Branch scope declaration idea: S/M/L with commit brackets
Session-end PR gate: flag if >15 commits not in a PR
State saving rules need to be canonized
13 cross-cutting subsystems
Configuration as single source of truth vs hardcoded values
Per-ecosystem required-vs-optional subsystem mapping needed
L5 is north star not exit criteria — most target L3-L4 baseline
Upgrade-friendliness as design principle in every component
MCP memory as secondary state backup (verify next session)
Configure episodic memory for remote sessions (broader benefit beyond this plan)
Interactivity-first as a design tenet for all skills and tooling — batch questioning > monologue output
Health report dual format (JSON envelope + JSONL findings) could become a general pattern for all ecosystem outputs
Core tenets discovery phase should precede schema/structure decisions — tenets guide everything downstream
Tenets should be CANON's first artifact (.canon/tenets.jsonl) — CANON defines itself before it defines others
CROSS_PLATFORM_SETUP.md is stale — references removed sync script. Needs update when platform-agnostic tenet is implemented
Cascade/override (D#5) and progressive disclosure are useful patterns but not tenets — keep as referenced patterns, not principles
17 tenets across 4 categories is the right granularity — specific enough to be actionable, broad enough to be stable

branch_investigation.reason: Squash-merged into main as PR #415 (commit 398904e). Zero diff between branch tip and main. All 6 branch commits are captured in the squash.

resume_instructions: Phase 0 APPROVED. Batches 1-4 COMPLETE (27 decisions). Batch 4T (Tenets Discovery) COMPLETE (5 new decisions: D28-D32, 32 total). 17 core tenets locked across 4 categories (Foundation: 3, Design: 4, Operations: 5, Process: 5). Includes platform-agnostic tenet (T7) for dual-environment constraint. Tenets to be stored as `.canon/tenets.jsonl` — first CANON artifact. Next: Batch 5 — Ecosystem Catalog + Maturity Assessments using the 16-item checklist against all 13 ecosystems + CANON. Consider sub-batches of 4-5 ecosystems for interactivity. Branch claude/system-standardization-discovery-s201 confirmed safe to delete (squash-merged as PR #415).
